proc:passivation.name: passivation
proc:passivation.description: Formation of a dense protective oxide film on the metal surface that prevents further reaction.

notes: When heated, passivation breaks down and the reaction proceeds
The reaction proceeds slowly due to the film; heating or amalgamation speeds it up

rule:passivation.destruction.base.method: Dissolving the oxide film in an alkali (Al₂O₃ is amphoteric)
rule:passivation.destruction.base.result: Al₂O₃ + 2NaOH → 2NaAlO₂ + H₂O, after which Al reacts with water

rule:passivation.destruction.amalgam.method: Amalgamation (contact with mercury destroys the film)

rule:passivation.destruction.heating.method: Heating (destroys passivation in conc. H₂SO₄ and HNO₃)
rule:passivation.destruction.heating.result: When heated, Fe, Al, Cr react with concentrated acids
